items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` **Service directory:** `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` **Triggered by:** PR #58520 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed The 1.14.0 release adopted a new `Azure.Core` version that includes identity types (e.g., `ClientSecretCredential`, `EnvironmentCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`) that were previously distributed exclusively in `Azure.Identity`. The `Azure.Identity` package is no longer a direct dependency. ## Gaps Found - The README code snippets use `ClientSecretCredential` and `EnvironmentCredential` without clarifying that these types now come from `Azure.Core` (≥ 1.53) — users may be confused about whether they still need to install `Azure.Identity` separately - The README does not have a **Prerequisites** subsection (required .NET version, whether `Azure.Identity` is still optional, etc.) - The README is missing the **Key concepts** section (explaining `AzureClientFactoryBuilder`, `IAzureClientFactory`, `AzureComponentFactory`, `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`) - The README is missing a **Troubleshooting** section - The README is missing a **Next steps** section
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md` 1. **Add a Prerequisites subsection** inside _Getting started_, after _Install the package_ and before _Register clients_: ```markdown ### Prerequisites - An [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted) - A compatible .NET runtime (see [Azure SDK for .NET target frameworks]((azure.github.io/redacted) > **Note:** Starting with version 1.14.0, authentication types such as `ClientSecretCredential`, `EnvironmentCredential`, and `ManagedIdentityCredential` are included in `Azure.Core` (≥ 1.53.0) and no longer require a separate `Azure.Identity` package reference. If you have an existing `Azure.Identity` reference in your project, it can be removed unless you use identity types not yet included in `Azure.Core`. ``` 2. **Add a Key concepts section** after the _Getting started_ section: ```markdown ## Key concepts - **`AddAzureClients`** – Extension method on `IServiceCollection` that registers one or more Azure SDK clients with the dependency injection container. - **`IAzureClientFactory`** – Interface that allows resolving named client instances at runtime. - **`AzureComponentFactory`** – Low-level factory for creating `TokenCredential`, `ClientOptions`, and client instances directly from `IConfiguration`. - **`AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`** – Bridges Azure SDK `EventSource`-based logs into the ASP.NET Core `ILogger` pipeline. Call `Start()` on startup and dispose on shutdown. - **Configuration-based credentials** – Clients can be constructed entirely from `IConfiguration` by setting well-known keys such as `credential`, `clientId`, `clientSecret`, `tenantId`, etc. See the [README credential reference](#register-clients) for supported values. ``` 3. **Add a Troubleshooting section** after _Examples_ / _Contributing_: ```markdown ## Troubleshooting - **`InvalidOperationException: Unable to find a constructor`** – Ensure the configuration section passed to `AddXxxClient` contains a key whose name matches a constructor parameter (e.g., `serviceUri` for `BlobServiceClient`). See [AzureComponentFactory]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - **Authentication failures** – Verify that the `credential` key in configuration matches one of the supported values (`default`, `environment`, `managedidentity`, `workloadidentity`, `azurepipelines`, `managedidentityasfederatedidentity`). Starting with 1.14.0, identity types are resolved from `Azure.Core`; remove any conflicting `Azure.Identity` references if you see `CS0433` ambiguous-reference errors. - **Logging** – Enable Azure SDK diagnostic logs in ASP.NET Core by calling `services.AddAzureClientsCore()` (which wires up `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder` automatically) or by manually starting an `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder` instance. ``` 4. **Add a Next steps section**: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Microsoft.Extensions.Azure API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Core credential types]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Dependency injection in ASP.NET Core]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet regions are required for the prerequisite note or the key concepts section — they are pure prose. The existing `ConfigureServices`, `Inject`, and `ResolveNamed` snippets remain valid. If you want to add a short snippet demonstrating the `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`, add a method to a new or existing sample file at: `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/tests/Samples/Sample_LogForwarder.cs` ````csharp #region Snippet:StartLogForwarder // Call once during application startup (e.g., inside Program.cs / Startup.cs) var logForwarder = app.Services.GetRequiredService(); logForwarder.Start(); #endregion ``` Then add a placeholder in the Troubleshooting or Key concepts section: ```` ````C# Snippet:StartLogForwarder ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current state of `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md`: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Implicit (covered in Register clients) — acceptable as-is | | Key concepts | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Examples | ⚠️ Covered inline under Getting started; could be a separate section but not blocking | | Troubleshooting | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Next steps | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 extensions` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; inspect git diff to confirm README updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 extensions` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Extensions] Docs: Update README for Azure.Identity → Azure.Core migration in 1.14.0
